definition:
1. a horse's hoof , (Hesiod):—;also, a cloven hoof , (Euripides) 
2. in plural, of the talons  of a bird, [variant datesTragica Adespota; of a wolf's claws , (Theocritus Poeta Bucolicus) 
3. a sea-bank, breakwater , formed of stones laid at the base of a sea-wall, to break the force of the waves (so called because it projected like a hoof) , Lat. crepido, (Thucydides) 
4. the spur of a mountain  or ridge of rocks  answering a like purpose, (Thucydides) 
5. a cloven implement , such as a netting-needle;—;  compare χηλεύω. (ML)
